change_protocol: edit catalog/schema source and declare compatibility impact
regenerate all projections in one packet or dependency-ordered stack
run parity diff and behavior fixtures
review public wording, security/capabilities and generated diff independently
merge only with one catalog/schema hash across surfaces
remove old path on clean cutover; no undocumented shim or alias
decision: A 45-operation core product catalog and its referenced record schemas are the public source of truth. bbh, OpenAPI, Python SDK, TypeScript SDK, TUI, and published documentation must project the same operation IDs and semantics; transport-specific extensions are explicit and cannot masquerade as core parity.

documentation_ownership.README: product-docs owner; one install-to-reverified-claim quickstart and honest claim ladder only
documentation_ownership.examples: owning module plus docs owner; every example is an executable CI fixture
documentation_ownership.how_to: deep-module owners co-own task journeys with product-docs owner; executable examples
documentation_ownership.openapi: API owner owns binding/runtime behavior; generated schema is checked output
documentation_ownership.python_sdk: SDK generator/template owner; no manual duplicate wire models
documentation_ownership.reference: generated from operation/record catalogs; contract owner owns source, direct edits forbidden
documentation_ownership.security_and_migration: security/contract owners; capability, secret, version and breaking-change rules
documentation_ownership.tui_help: TUI owner; generated operation links plus hand-authored interaction guidance
documentation_ownership.typescript_sdk: SDK generator/template owner; no manual duplicate wire models

generation_and_verification_pipeline: validate active schemas and operation/record catalogs
generate canonical Python and TypeScript models plus low-level clients
generate/check normalized OpenAPI schema refs and operation IDs against catalog bindings
generate/check CLI command inventory and reference help
generate/check TUI typed action inventory and SDK usage
generate reference documentation tables and surface matrix
run set/schema/policy parity comparator across all six inventories
run common behavioral conformance fixtures against local core, HTTP, Python SDK and TypeScript SDK
run CLI journey and TUI E2E journey against the same fixture/build
fail if regeneration changes the checkout
non_claims: a generated client proving server behavior
README coverage substituting for executable examples
TUI screenshots substituting for operation/state evidence

facts: bbh exposes useful harness and lane subsets but not the frozen complete journey
the current E4 router is predominantly read-only and exposes claim reverification as its only E4 mutation
the Python SDK root exports heterogeneous runtime helpers rather than one canonical product-operation client
the TypeScript packages and barrel exports do not form one complete operation surface
the TUI API client does not cover the complete frozen journey
the README primary journey does not carry an outsider through executable E4 claim reverification

operation_semantics.artifact: read and verify immutable artifacts; arbitrary public artifact writes are excluded
operation_semantics.claim: claims are created only by lane.claim after comparison; get/list/evidence/reverify never manufacture support
operation_semantics.harness: definitions are mutable only before lock; validate and explain do not mutate; start consumes a lock
operation_semantics.integration: read/probe provider routes, tools, resources, host drivers, and capture adapters; credential values remain host-specific and non-public
operation_semantics.lane: author/lock then either composed lane.run or the identical inspectable capture/normalize/replay/compare/claim stages
operation_semantics.session: one runtime execution with observable events, explicit operator input/approval, resume, and cancellation

rules: operation ID is identical in every machine-readable surface inventory
public handler/command/method/action without an active catalog entry fails the gate
catalog entry without all six projections fails the gate
breaking semantic change requires versioned operation/schema plus human approval

parity_gate.behavior_rule: each operation has shared contract fixtures; every mutating/async operation is exercised through all applicable executable clients, and TUI/CLI complete the full frozen journey
parity_gate.documentation_rule: 100% operation/record index coverage; journey examples execute on the release candidate
parity_gate.freshness: all inventories, OpenAPI, clients, TUI, docs and evidence bind the same candidate head/catalog/schema hashes
parity_gate.quality_rule: zero schema/generator drift, zero stale generated files, zero unresolved P0/P1/P2/Q findings
parity_gate.record_rule: 100% active public schema models generate, round-trip and hash identically in Python and TypeScript
parity_gate.review: independent reviewer approves exact hashes; CI pass alone is insufficient
parity_gate.set_rule: all 45 core operation IDs appear exactly once in each of the six inventories; no unclassified public extras

rules: one role has one active public schema version at a time
operation inputs/outputs/errors/events reference schema IDs; no inline duplicate models
Python/TypeScript/OpenAPI models are generated projections, never independent definitions
cross-language canonical JSON/hash fixtures are mandatory
additional/internal fields require a new schema version rather than silent widening
record_source_of_truth.schemas: versioned JSON Schema 2020-12 files are canonical for wire shape, validation, canonicalization and hashes
record_source_of_truth.selection: contracts/public/record_surface.v1.json names the exact active schema IDs for each required role

required_record_roles: Provider Route Lock and capability probe
tool call and Tool Execution Outcome

bbh.inventory: bbh system surface --json emits catalog hash and bindings
bbh.shape: resource-oriented nouns and verbs; global --json emits bb.cli.result.v1; local and --server modes have identical core outcomes

docs.contract: every operation and record role has one generated reference entry and appears in the surface index; journey-critical operations also have an executed narrative example
docs.inventory: generated reference index keyed by operation/schema ID

openapi.async: 202 plus canonical execution/session resource and event stream where operation catalog marks async
openapi.inventory: normalized OpenAPI operation IDs and schema refs
openapi.shape: resource paths for harnesses, locks, sessions, integrations, lanes/executions/stages, claims, artifacts and system

python_sdk.inventory: generated package manifest with operation and schema IDs
python_sdk.naming: snake_case methods grouped by deep module; every method carries canonical operation_id metadata
python_sdk.types: generated from public JSON Schemas/OpenAPI; ergonomic facades may only delegate

tui.accessibility: all actions keyboard reachable with status/error/next-action output
tui.client: uses the generated TypeScript SDK, not handwritten fetch shapes
tui.contract: each canonical operation has a reachable action or read view; raw state/report/hash/error is inspectable even when the primary interaction is visual
tui.inventory: typed action registry keyed by canonical operation ID

typescript_sdk.inventory: generated package manifest with operation and schema IDs
typescript_sdk.naming: camelCase methods grouped by deep module; every method carries canonical operationId metadata
typescript_sdk.types: generated from public JSON Schemas/OpenAPI; root barrel exports every public client/type

examples: HTTP auth-token exchange and WebSocket handshake
rules: declared in a separate extension registry
cannot change a core operation's inputs, outcomes, gates, or evidence language
security-sensitive secret values never enter the core operation catalog
